2606.07929.title: Stress-testing medical large language models reveals latent safety pathology beyond benchmark accuracy
2606.07929.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are entering clinical practice based on benchmark accuracy that may fail to detect safety-relevant failure modes. Here we present AI-MASLD, a stress-audit framework that adapts the logic of metabolic stress testing from hepatology to the evaluation of clinical LLMs. Using 240 clinical cases across six narrative perturbation probes, we subjected seven models to double-stress testing and quantified performance through three indices: metabolic index (MI), perturbation flip rate (PFR), and counterfactual fairness index (CFI). Under clean baseline conditions, all models performed uniformly well. Under realistic narrative stress, performance diverged sharply, revealing two distinct stress-response phenotypes. Quantized models exhibited pseudonormalization, in which low flip rates hid functional collapse. Medical supervised fine-tuning systematically degraded logical stability, fairness, and information extraction. An open-weight model matched or exceeded proprietary alternatives on every safety dimension. These findings establish narrative stress auditing as a necessary complement to accuracy-based evaluation.
2606.07929.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）正在根據基準準確性進入臨床實踐，但這可能無法檢測到與安全相關的失效模式。<lb/>在此，我們提出了AI-MASLD，一個壓力審核框架，將肝臟病學中的代謝壓力測試邏輯調整為臨床LLMs的評估。<lb/>使用240個臨床案例和六個敘事擾動探針，我們對七個模型進行了雙重壓力測試，並通過三個指標量化性能：代謝指數（MI）、擾動翻轉率（PFR）和反事實公平指數（CFI）。<lb/>在乾淨的基線條件下，所有模型的表現均一致良好。<lb/>在現實的敘事壓力下，性能急劇分化，顯示出兩種不同的壓力反應表型。<lb/>量化模型表現出偽正常化，其中低翻轉率掩蓋了功能崩潰。<lb/>醫學監督的微調系統性地降低了邏輯穩定性、公平性和信息提取。<lb/>開放權重模型在每個安全維度上均與專有替代品相匹配或超越。<lb/>這些發現確立了敘事壓力審核作為基於準確性評估的必要補充。
2606.07929.author: Yuan Shen et.al.
2606.07929.authors: Yuan Shen, Xiaojun Wu, Linghua Yu
